pi_items.verified_value: Valid coordinates within Solent strait, approximately 2.8 nm north of central Solent (50.7853°N, -1.2903°W)
pi_items.source: Web search: Solent coordinates from latitude.to; verified geographic location in Southampton Water/Solent region

pi_items.verified_value: Valid coordinates within Solent strait, west of collection point by 0.010°
pi_items.source: Geographic validation; part of symmetric V-shaped geometry

pi_items.verified_value: Valid coordinates within Solent strait, east of collection point by 0.010°
pi_items.source: Geographic validation; part of symmetric V-shaped geometry

pi_items.verified_value: 0.002 degrees of latitude = 0.12 nautical miles = approximately 222 meters
pi_items.source: Mathematical conversion using standard nautical calculations (1° lat = 60 nm, 1 nm = 1852 m)

pi_items.verified_value: Correct geographic region. Solent is the strait between Isle of Wight and mainland England (Hampshire), approximately 20 miles long and 2.5-5 miles wide. Central coordinates: 50.7853°N, -1.2903°W. Bordered by New Forest National Park.
pi_items.source: Web search: Wikipedia and latitude.to articles on Solent

pi_items.verified_value: Southampton Water is a tidal estuary north of Solent at approximately 50.8537°N, -1.3418°W. Task coordinates are within Solent region and relatively close to Southampton Water center (approximately 1.8 nm away)
pi_items.source: Web search: Southampton Water coordinates from latitude.to article

pi_items.metadata_value: Red buoys (Buoy_R) for port hand marks
pi_items.verified_value: UK uses IALA Region A buoyage system where red buoys mark port hand lateral marks. Consistent with IALA conventions.
pi_items.source: Web search: IALA Region A buoyage system articles (marineinsight.com, safe-skipper.com, Trinity House)

pi_items.verified_value: West arm and east arm are geometrically symmetric: both tips at same latitude (50.8250°N), equidistant from collection point (0.010° longitude each), converging to collection point at same latitude differential (0.010°)

With collection point to tip latitude span of 0.010°, expected approximately 5 buoys per arm (0.010 / 0.002 = 5). Total V-shape horizontal span approximately 0.76 nautical miles (0.020° longitude at latitude 50.825°)

privileged_info_summary: The oil spill boom deployment task uses real geographic coordinates from the Solent strait in southern England (50.8350°N, -1.3050°W collection point, with symmetric V-shaped arms extending to 50.8250°N at longitudes -1.3150°W and -1.2950°W). The coordinates place the scenario approximately 1.8 nautical miles from Southampton Water center and within the Solent region bordered by the New Forest. The task creates a synthetic scenario in Bridge Command simulator using IALA Region A buoyage conventions (where red buoys mark port hand marks, appropriate for UK waters). The geometry is mathematically sound: the V-shaped boom arms are symmetric, with buoys spaced at 0.002° latitude intervals (approximately 222 meters or 0.12 nautical miles apart), yielding approximately 5 buoys per arm across a 0.010° latitude span. The total horizontal span of the V-shape is approximately 0.76 nautical miles.
